# `@bilbomusic/player-plugin-sdk`

The official JavaScript SDK for creating custom player skins, visual themes, and interactive visualizer extensions within the [Bilbo Music](https://bilbomusic.com) music platform ecosystem.

This library handles secure, isolated, and high-performance cross-origin data synchronization between the main Bilbo Music host player application and your custom plugin running inside a sandboxed `<iframe>`. It abstracts low-level `postMessage` exchanges into a developer-friendly, event-driven API for managing audio state, tracking playlist queues, and rendering real-time frequency data.

---

## üì¶ Installation

Install the package into your custom player plugin project repository via any JavaScript node package manager:

```bash
npm install @bilbomusic/player-plugin-sdk
# or
yarn add @bilbomusic/player-plugin-sdk
# or
pnpm add @bilbomusic/player-plugin-sdk
```

---

## üöÄ Quick Start

Import the `playerSdk` singleton module directly into your bundle. The library instantly invokes an automated handshake layer signaling connectivity back to the **Bilbo Music** application core.

```javascript
import { playerSdk } from '@bilbomusic/player-plugin-sdk';

// 1. Declaratively hide native player UI nodes to map out your layout
playerSdk.setUiConfig({
    carousel: false,    // Hide the host's cover art viewer to draw a custom one
    progressBar: false, // Disable the host trackbar to use an interactive Waveform tracker
    interactive: true   // Authorize pointer events on the iframe so our buttons work
});

// 2. Attach a generalized reactive observer tracking screen mutation parameters
playerSdk.on('change', ({ type, payload }) => {
    if (type === 'track') {
        console.log('Now playing:', payload.track.title);
        document.getElementById('track-title').innerText = payload.track.title;
    }
    if (type === 'position') {
        console.log('Current playback milestone (ms):', payload.position);
    }
}, 'default');

// 3. Delegate actions seamlessly back to the hardware device audio pipeline
document.getElementById('playBtn').addEventListener('click', () => {
    playerSdk.play();
});
```

---

## üìë Event Bus Specification

Register and tear down hook listeners using `.on(event, callback, pane)` and `.off(event, callback, pane)` handlers.

### Global System Events
These hooks run continuously outside the boundaries of contextual pane view layouts:
*   `init`: Fires exactly once upon bootstrap handshake termination. Transmits the absolute current root `state` configuration object.
*   `theme`: Dispatched when the environment triggers systemic dark/light skin adjustments. Emits `{ theme: 'light' | 'dark' }`.
*   `locale`: Dispatched when regional runtime changes occur. Returns a safe layout configuration schema: `{ locale: string, rtl: boolean }`.
*   `player`: Signals changes regarding core display modes. Returns `{ expanded: boolean }`.

### Scoped Pane Events
Bound exclusively to independent application UI screens (defaults to `pane = 'default'`).
*   `change`: The primary atomic multi-property observer for theme developers. Fires when *any* variable modifies within the active viewport profile. Returns an argument matching the structure: `{ type: string, payload: any, pane: string }`.
*   `audioFrame`: Stream gate dispatching digital signal audio frequency bands for spectrum analyzer canvases. Fires at up to ~60 frames per second. Yields a single `Uint8Array` binary mapping.
*   You may also attach explicit hooks listening strictly to a specific variable name instead of checking the global aggregate tracker (e.g. subscribing directly to events named `'track'`, `'position'`, `'state'`, or `'queue'`).

---

## üõÝÔ∏è API Reference Methods

### Playback Automation
*   `playerSdk.play(pane)` ‚Äî Resumes active media stream decoding.
*   `playerSdk.pause(pane)` ‚Äî Halts audio rendering at the current frame milestone.
*   `playerSdk.seek(ms, pane)` ‚Äî Sets playback runtime forward or backward to an explicit milestone in milliseconds (`ms` must be a valid `number`).
*   `playerSdk.next(pane)` ‚Äî Skips ahead to the next logical sequence block item.
*   `playerSdk.prev(pane)` ‚Äî Jumps back toward the preceding asset record.

### Container Overlays & Layouts
*   `playerSdk.expand()` ‚Äî Prompts the parent viewport frame to transition into a full-height, expanded view configuration layer.
*   `playerSdk.collapse()` ‚Äî Minimizes the parent frame bounds into a compact mini-player bar profile layout.
*   `playerSdk.openTrackKebab()` ‚Äî Triggers the opening of the native contextual tracks feature card/modal dialog overview.
*   `playerSdk.openPlaylist()` ‚Äî Displays the global slide-out queue overlay screen list container.
*   `playerSdk.setUiConfig(config)` ‚Äî Accepts a structural config `Object` modifying the visibility state flags of parent core blocks surrounding the plugin node wrapper.

### Queue & Metadata Modifications
*   `playerSdk.playQueueIndex(index, pane)` ‚Äî Instructs the streaming backend to break current execution order and mount the sequential entity track matching the specified zero-based array position value (`index`).
*   `playerSdk.reaction(reaction, pane)` ‚Äî Passes user preference validation indicators directly to backend endpoints (`'LIKE'`, `'DISLIKE'`, `null`, or `''`).
*   `playerSdk.repeat(repeat, pane)` ‚Äî Modifies active collection repeat modes (`'none'`, `'queue'`, `'track'`).

---

## üìä Data Formats & DTO (Data Transfer Objects)

The following type definitions document the incoming payloads dispatched down from the Bilbo Music environment into the player plugin runtime.

### `Image`
A comprehensive asset record managing pre-rendered variant dimensions to enforce optimized RAM consumption inside the client web wrapper.
*   `crop`: `string` ‚Äî Uniform cropped thumbnail endpoint mapping exactly to **180x180** dimensions. Recommended for timeline history line items, list indices, and side queues.
*   `resized`: `string` ‚Äî High-definition compressed variant mapping to **1000x1000** properties. Best suited for focal backgrounds and center cover art cards.
*   `original`: `string` ‚Äî Full-resolution fallback referencing the uncompressed source matrix asset directly.

### `ArtistPreview`
*   `name`: `string` ‚Äî Public identity string or stage name representing the performer profile.
*   `code`: `string` ‚Äî Systemic alphanumeric slug mapping the performer uniquely inside route configurations.
*   `profileImage`: `Image` ‚Äî Multi-tier image record referencing portrait asset arrays.

### `TrackPreview`
*   `title`: `string` ‚Äî The primary presentation name string of the track.
*   `artists`: `string` ‚Äî Pre-formatted utility string concatenating multiple composer identities into a readable line.
*   `duration`: `number` ‚Äî Total playback length value evaluated exclusively in **milliseconds**.
*   `bpm`: `number | null` ‚Äî Measured speed metrics tracking structural beats per minute.
*   `wave`: `number[]` ‚Äî Array containing **exactly 100 integer positions** mapping static track sound amplitude volumes. Theoretical density calculations span from `0` up to `1000`, though real-world production track master peaks safely average within the `500‚Äì700` threshold limits. Useful for drawing custom audio waveforms that act as full-length interactive scrubbars.
*   `isExplicit`: `boolean` ‚Äî Content indicator denoting whether structural components feature restricted lyrical content.
*   `version`: `string | null` ‚Äî Supplemental performance label strings (e.g. `"Remix"`, `"Radio Edit"`, `"Acoustic Mix"`).
*   `fromProject`: `boolean` ‚Äî System tag validating native Bilbo-exclusive resource distribution tracks.
*   `cover`: `Image` ‚Äî Multi-scale picture payload referencing track artwork maps.
*   `contributors`: `Object` ‚Äî Granular profile map tracking credits:
    *   `primary`: `ArtistPreview[]` ‚Äî Core headline performer entities.
    *   `featured`: `ArtistPreview[]` ‚Äî Guest performers contributing to the specific composition.

### `ReleasePreview`
*   `title`: `string` ‚Äî Legal name tracking collective works (Albums, Box sets, Compilation EPs).
*   `cover`: `Image` ‚Äî Graphic reference catalog mapping release compilation packaging artwork.
*   `fromProject`: `boolean` ‚Äî Identifies compilation packages associated with internal project tags.
*   `artists`: `string` ‚Äî Combined string tracking project grouping identities.
*   `contributors`: `Object` ‚Äî Structural performer attribution map definitions:
    *   `primary`: `ArtistPreview[]`
    *   `featured`: `ArtistPreview[]`

### `PlaylistPreview`
*   `name`: `string` ‚Äî Display moniker given to the designated digital collection folder record.
*   `title`: `string` ‚Äî Optional description subtext or alternative header mapping.
*   `code`: `string` ‚Äî Unique systemic reference identifier matching database route queries.
*   `type`: `string` ‚Äî Operational metadata category categorization tags (e.g. `"user"`, `"editorial"`, `"smart"`).
*   `cover`: `Image` ‚Äî Compilation artwork schema tracking active header layout designs.

### `QueuePreview`
The aggregate array configuration documenting upcoming performance items loaded into the live execution stack buffer.
*   `items`: `TrackPreview[]` ‚Äî Active sequence list tracking tracks prepared for chronologically ordered playback execution.
*   `playlist`: `PlaylistPreview | null` ‚Äî Origin track context mapping if the sequence pipeline initialized out of a shared collection list folder.
*   `release`: `ReleasePreview | null` ‚Äî Origin studio record context map if the pipeline was populated from an Album or single release container.
*   `artist`: `ArtistPreview | null` ‚Äî Identity mapping reference if the playlist pipeline query was spawned dynamically out of an explicit artist page dashboard.

---

## üí° Practical Implementation Snippets

#### 1. Rendering an Interactive Waveform Progress Scrub Bar
Using the array inside `track.wave` paired with real-time `position` updates, you can draw a custom timeline with HTML bars or a Canvas context:

```javascript
playerSdk.on('change', ({ type, payload }) => {
    if (type === 'track') {
        const waveData = payload.track.wave; // Returns exactly 100 amplitude values
        renderWaveforms(waveData); // Spawn 100 stylized custom layout columns
    }
});
```

#### 2. Enhancing Queue Layout Rendering Speeds
When constructing sidebars listing long indices inside `QueuePreview`, optimize layout processing cycles by binding your structural image source elements directly onto the compact asset `crop` endpoint:

```javascript
const populateQueueSidebar = (queuePayload) => {
    queuePayload.items.forEach(trackItem => {
        const thumbNode = document.createElement('img');
        // Binding onto crop (180x180) reduces frame execution cost inside the iframe sandboxed memory bounds
        thumbNode.src = trackItem.cover?.crop || 'fallback-artwork.png'; 
        document.body.appendChild(thumbNode);
    });
};
```
